formation_and_validity.Section 2(h): Contract is an agreement enforceable by law. An agreement not enforceable by law is void
formation_and_validity.Section 10: What agreements are contracts. All agreements are contracts if they are made by free consent of parties competent to contract, for a lawful consideration and with a lawful object, and are not expressly declared to be void
formation_and_validity.Section 11: Who are competent to contract. Every person is competent to contract who is of the age of majority, is of sound mind, and is not disqualified from contracting by any law
formation_and_validity.Section 13: Consent is said to be free when it is not caused by coercion, undue influence, fraud, misrepresentation, or mistake
formation_and_validity.Section 15: Coercion is the committing or threatening to commit any act forbidden by the Indian Penal Code or the unlawful detaining or threatening to detain any property
formation_and_validity.Section 16: Undue influence. A contract is said to be induced by undue influence where the relations between the parties are such that one party is in a position to dominate the will of the other
formation_and_validity.Section 17: Fraud means and includes any act committed by a party to a contract with intent to deceive the other party or to induce him to enter into the contract. Includes suggestion of fact that is not true, active concealment of fact, promise without intention of performing, any other act fitted to deceive, any such act or omission as the law specially declares to be fraudulent
formation_and_validity.Section 19: Voidability of agreements without free consent. When consent is caused by coercion, fraud or misrepresentation, the agreement is voidable at the option of the party whose consent was so caused
formation_and_validity.Section 23: Lawful consideration and object. Consideration or object of an agreement is unlawful if it is forbidden by law, would defeat the provisions of any law, is fraudulent, involves injury to person or property, or is opposed to public policy
formation_and_validity.Section 25: Agreement without consideration is void unless it is made on account of natural love and affection in writing, or is a promise to compensate for something done, or is a promise to pay a time-barred debt

performance_and_breach.Section 37: Obligation of parties to contracts. The parties to a contract must either perform or offer to perform their respective promises
performance_and_breach.Section 56: Agreement to do impossible act is void. Contract to do an act which after the contract is made becomes impossible or unlawful becomes void (doctrine of frustration)
performance_and_breach.Section 73: Compensation for loss or damage caused by breach of contract. When a contract has been broken, the party who suffers is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach. Remote and indirect loss not recoverable
performance_and_breach.Section 74: Compensation for breach of contract where penalty stipulated. When a contract has been broken, if a sum is named in the contract as the amount to be paid or penalty, the party complaining of breach is entitled to receive reasonable compensation not exceeding the amount named
performance_and_breach.Section 75: Party rightfully rescinding contract is entitled to compensation for any damage he has sustained through the non-fulfilment of the contract

agency.Section 182: Agent is a person employed to do any act for another or to represent another in dealings with third persons. The person for whom the act is done is called the principal
agency.Section 185: No consideration is necessary to create an agency
agency.Section 188: Extent of agent's authority. An agent having authority to do an act has authority to do every lawful thing which is necessary in order to do such act
agency.Section 196: Right of person dealing with agent personally liable. If agent contracts for principal with agent's knowledge, the other contracting party may hold either agent or principal liable
agency.Section 201: Termination of agency. An agency is terminated by the principal revoking his authority, by the agent renouncing, by the business being completed, by death or insanity
